subject: Science
chapter: Adolescence: Stage of Growth and Change

activities_projects.title: Growth Chart Tracking
activities_projects.objective: Observe physical growth patterns during adolescence
steps: Measure your current height in centimeters
Collect height data of 5 friends (both boys and girls)
Compare the heights and note who is growing faster at this age
Plot a simple bar graph showing the variation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that sudden increase in height is a key sign of adolescence
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate height measurements and graph representation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Secondary Sexual Characteristics Checklist
activities_projects.objective: Identify physical changes occurring during puberty
steps: Read the section on secondary sexual characteristics
Create a table with two columns: 'Changes in Boys' and 'Changes in Girls'
List at least 4 changes for each (e.g., voice change, hair growth, etc.)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the physical markers of puberty in different genders
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct listing of gender-specific physical changes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Adam's Apple Observation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the change in voice during adolescence
steps: Observe the throat area in the mirror while talking or swallowing
Note if there is a protrusion (Adam's apple)
Discuss why boys often develop a hoarse or deep voice during this stage
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the role of the larynx in voice changes for adolescent boys
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear explanation of the development of the voice box (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Healthy Habit Poster
activities_projects.objective: Promote the importance of personal hygiene during adolescence
steps: Research why sweat and oil glands become more active at this age
Draw three habits for maintaining hygiene (e.g., daily bath, washing face)
Write a slogan for the poster
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns the necessity of hygiene due to increased gland activity
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance of the hygiene tips (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Balanced Diet Planner
activities_projects.objective: Understand nutritional needs during the growth spurt
steps: List the food items you ate yesterday
Identify which items provide proteins, carbohydrates, and fats
Create a 'Balanced Meal Plan' specifically for a growing teenager
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Realizes the importance of nutrition for proper physical development
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the balanced diet chart (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hormone Match-up
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the endocrine glands and their secretions
steps: Make cards for glands (Pituitary, Thyroid, Adrenal, Pancreas, Testes/Ovaries)
Make cards for hormones (Growth hormone, Thyroxine, Adrenaline, Insulin, Testosterone/Estrogen)
Match the gland to its correct hormone and function
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies key hormones responsible for adolescent changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the matched pairs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Emotional Well-being Diary
activities_projects.objective: Understand the emotional and mental changes during adolescence
steps: Write down three different moods or feelings you experienced this week
Discuss with a peer or elder why feeling confused or self-conscious is normal
List one way to stay positive (like a hobby or sport)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that mental and emotional shifts are a normal part of growth
activities_projects.evaluation: Reflection on emotional changes and coping strategies (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Say No to Drugs Campaign
activities_projects.objective: Awareness about the dangers of substance abuse during adolescence
steps: Research why the adolescent brain is vulnerable to addiction
Create a 'Say No' card explaining that drugs ruin health and future
Present it to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops a strong stance against harmful substances
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the message and awareness level (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Myths vs. Facts Table
steps: List 3 common myths about growth or menstruation
Write the scientific fact next to each myth
Explain why it is important to believe in science over rumors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Clears up misinformation regarding adolescent development
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to distinguish between myth and scientific fact (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Model Interview
activities_projects.objective: Learn from others' experiences of growing up
steps: Interview an older sibling or parent about their teenage years
Ask about the biggest change they noticed in themselves
Summarize the interview in five points
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Builds perspective on the transition from childhood to adulthood
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of interview questions and summary (10 marks)
